_comment: Citation database. Every numeric claim in chips.json and processes.json carries a sourceId referencing an entry here.

sources.title: DTCO including Sustainability: Power-Performance-Area-Cost-Environmental score (PPACE) Analysis for Logic Technologies
sources.authors: M. Garcia Bardon, P. Wuytens, L.-Å. Ragnarsson, et al.
sources.keyFindings: From N28 to N2, per-wafer increases: electricity x3.46, ultrapure water x2.3, GHG x2.5. Environmental cost dominated by etch and lithography at advanced nodes. NF3 abatement factor assumed 95%.

sources.title: How Can We Reduce Environmental Impact in Chip Manufacturing?
sources.keyFindings: An advanced N2 logic node generates approximately 1,600 kg CO2eq per wafer. Dry etch and lithography together contribute nearly 40% of total emissions. Scope 2 (electricity) emissions are ~60% of total carbon footprint at advanced nodes. IC manufacturing accounts for ~185 Mt CO2e/year globally.

sources.title: Sustainable Semiconductor Technologies and Systems (SSTS) Program
sources.keyFindings: IC manufacturing projected to reach 3% of global GHG emissions by 2040 if unchecked. NF3 has GWP 17,000x higher than CO2 (100-yr horizon). Cleaning and etching identified as primary GHG-emitting process families.

sources.title: Environmental data and facts in the semiconductor manufacturing industry: An unexpected high water and energy consumption situation
sources.keyFindings: 2021 industry averages (normalized per cm2 of silicon): water 8.22 L/cm2, energy 1.15 kWh/cm2, GHG 0.84 kg CO2eq/cm2. Total 2021 industry footprint: 7.89x10^8 m3 water, 1.49x10^11 kWh energy, 7.15x10^7 tons CO2eq.

sources.title: 2024 TSMC Sustainability Report
sources.keyFindings: Wastewater recycling exceeded 140 million m3 in 2024. Water recycling rate >95%. Unit wafer water footprint reduced vs 2020 for both mature and advanced fabs. Targets 60% renewable energy by 2030, RE100 by 2040.

sources.title: The Environmental Footprint of Logic CMOS Technologies
sources.keyFindings: Ultrapure water used for rinsing (wet benches) and CMP steps, which double in count from node to node. NF3 and SF6 are main F-gas contributors. EUV consumes more power per tool but reduces multi-patterning steps; net energy depends on throughput.

sources.title: Semiconductor Industry — Fluorinated Gas Partnership
sources.keyFindings: Fluorinated GHGs used: CF4, C2F6, C3F8, c-C4F8, CHF3, CH3F, CH2F2, NF3, SF6. 10-80% of F-gas passes through unreacted without abatement. Facilities emitting ≥25,000 tCO2e/year from F-gas must report under 40 CFR Part 98 Subpart I.

sources.title: Plasma Etch and Deposition White Paper
sources.keyFindings: NF3 GWP100 = 17,400. 3D NAND etching involves >100 dielectric layers, projected >200. SSD manufacturing emits 6-7x more Scope 1+2 GHG than HDD. Ratio of gases emitted vs used has dropped significantly 2000-2020 due to abatement, but plateauing 2010-2020.

sources.title: Fluorinated greenhouse gas and net-zero emissions from the electronics industry: the proof is in the pudding
sources.keyFindings: FC emissions are 80-90% of uncontrolled direct (Scope 1) emissions. 85-90% of FC emissions (in CO2e) originate from plasma-based etching and cleaning. SF6 GWP100 = 25,200. CF4 atmospheric lifetime ~50,000 years.

sources.title: Semiconductor Emission Explorer: Tracking GHG Emissions from Chip Production (2015-2023)
sources.keyFindings: NF3 accounted for 22% of total F-gas emissions in 2020. Memory chip production requires higher amounts of F-gases due to high aspect ratio etching (3D NAND). F-gases account for 80-90% of direct emissions in a fab.

sources.title: NVIDIA Hopper Architecture In-Depth
sources.keyFindings: GH100 (H100): TSMC 4N (custom N4) process, 80 billion transistors, 814 mm2 die. 700W SXM5 TDP.

sources.title: Apple A18 — Wikipedia (compiled from Apple and TechInsights data)
sources.keyFindings: A18: 90 mm2, A18 Pro: 105 mm2. Both on TSMC N3E. A18 Pro estimated >20 billion transistors.

sources.title: Zen 4 — Wikipedia (compiled from AMD and TechPowerUp)
sources.keyFindings: Zen 4 CCD (used in Ryzen 7000 and EPYC Genoa): 66.3 mm2 (some sources 70 mm2), TSMC N5, 6.5 billion transistors per CCD. EPYC Genoa can contain up to 12 CCDs + a ~397 mm2 IOD on N6.

sources.title: Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 specs
sources.keyFindings: Snapdragon 8 Gen 3: TSMC N4P, 8-core CPU (1x Cortex-X4 + 5x A720 + 2x A520). Die size estimated ~120 mm2.

sources.title: Apple M4 Architecture and Die Size Analysis
sources.keyFindings: The Apple M4 is fabricated on TSMC's N3E 3nm process. It features 28 billion transistors and has a measured die area of approximately 165.9 mm^2. The architecture utilizes 10 CPU cores and a 10-core GPU, supported by unified memory.

sources.title: NVIDIA reveals die sizes for GB200 Blackwell GPUs
sources.keyFindings: The flagship Blackwell GPU die (GB202) features a 750 mm^2 die size with 92.2 billion transistors. It is built on a custom TSMC 4NP node. For enterprise B200 configurations, two dies are connected via a 10 TB/s interconnect to pack 208 billion transistors.

sources.title: Meteor Lake - Intel 4 Node and Die Size Details
sources.org: IEEE VLSI Symposium / Tom's Hardware / Wikipedia
sources.keyFindings: The Meteor Lake compute tile is fabricated on the 'Intel 4' node (Intel's first EUV node, 7nm class). The CPU compute tile measures 69.67 mm^2. It is packaged via Foveros 3D packaging alongside graphics, SoC, and I/O tiles.

sources.title: Qualcomm Snapdragon X Elite Die Exposed and Annotated
sources.keyFindings: The Snapdragon X Elite die measures 169.6 mm2 and is fabricated using TSMC's N4P 4 nm-class process. It features a 12-core Oryon CPU and a 45 TOPS NPU.

sources.title: Google Tensor G4 - Digital Floorplan Analysis & Spec Overview
sources.keyFindings: Fabricated on Samsung's 4nm process (4LPP+). Features 1x Cortex-X4, 3x Cortex-A720, and 4x Cortex-A520 cores alongside a dedicated third-generation Tensor Processing Unit for edge AI tasks.

sources.title: AMD MI300X vs NVIDIA H200: Memory, Performance, and Cost for AI Workloads
sources.keyFindings: The AMD Instinct MI300X features 153 billion transistors across 8 GPU compute dies (5nm) and 3 I/O dies (6nm). It utilizes 192 GB of HBM3 memory providing 5.3 TB/s of bandwidth, designed specifically for heavy AI training and inference workloads with a 750W TDP.

sources.title: TPU v5p - Google Cloud Documentation
sources.keyFindings: TPU v5p delivers 459 TFLOPs of peak compute (BF16/FP8) per chip. A full TPU v5p Pod contains 8,960 chips connected via a 3D torus topology. Each chip includes 95 GiB of HBM capacity with 2575 GiBps bandwidth.

sources.title: NVIDIA AD102 'Ada' Packs Over 75 Billion Transistors
sources.keyFindings: NVIDIA's AD102 GPU is built on the TSMC N5 (5 nm EUV) node. It features a transistor count of 76.3 billion and a die area of 608 mm^2. The N5 node offers 134 MTr/mm^2, significantly increasing density over the previous 8nm node.

sources.title: Transistor count: Microprocessors
sources.keyFindings: The Apple M3 Max is a 16-core 64-bit ARM64 SoC fabricated on TSMC's 3nm (N3B) process. It contains 92,000,000,000 transistors. Die size is estimated around 320 mm^2.

sources.title: Raptor Lake Architecture and Die Details
sources.keyFindings: Raptor Lake is fabricated using the Intel 7 process (formerly 10nm Enhanced SuperFin). The top-end desktop die (e.g., 13900K) features 8 P-cores and 16 E-cores. Transistor count is approximately 14.2 billion on a 257 mm^2 die.

sources.title: Cerebras Wafer-Scale Engine: When to Choose Alternative AI Architecture
sources.keyFindings: The WSE-3 is built on a TSMC 5nm process node. It occupies an entire usable wafer with a die area of 46,225 mm^2 (21.5 cm x 21.5 cm). It contains 4 trillion transistors, 900,000 AI cores, and 44 GB of on-chip SRAM.

sources.title: AMD's 3D V-Cache Deep Dive
sources.keyFindings: AMD's 3D V-Cache SRAM die is built on TSMC's 7nm node. It measures 36 mm^2 and contains 4.7 billion transistors. It is bonded via direct copper-to-copper hybrid bonding over the Zen 3/Zen 4 compute dies.

sources.title: Tesla Details Dojo Supercomputer Architecture
sources.keyFindings: The Tesla D1 chip is fabricated on TSMC's 7nm node. It packs 50 billion transistors into a massive 645 mm^2 die. It is optimized purely for machine learning workloads, bypassing traditional caches for high-speed SRAM.

sources.title: Transistor count: Mobile Processors
sources.keyFindings: The Apple A17 Pro (used in iPhone 15 Pro) is built on TSMC's 3nm process (N3B). It contains 19 billion transistors and has a measured die area of 103.8 mm^2, resulting in a density of approximately 183 MTr/mm^2.

sources.title: MediaTek Dimensity 9300 All-Big-Core Architecture
sources.keyFindings: Built on TSMC's N4P (4nm-class) process, featuring a unique CPU configuration of 4x Cortex-X4 and 4x Cortex-A720. Die size is estimated at 135 mm^2 with 22.7 billion transistors.

sources.title: Samsung Exynos 2400 Deep Dive
sources.keyFindings: The Exynos 2400 utilizes Samsung Foundry's 4LPP+ node. It features a 10-core CPU configuration and an RDNA3-based Xclipse 940 GPU, containing roughly 18.5 billion transistors.

sources.title: Intel Alder Lake Architecture and Die Sizes
sources.keyFindings: The Alder Lake-P (mobile) die is built on the Intel 7 node. It measures 217 mm^2 and contains an estimated 10.5 billion transistors, packing 6 Golden Cove P-cores and 8 Gracemont E-cores.
